root_text.an7.26:1.3: katame ca, bhikkhave, satta aparihāniyā dhammā?
root_text.an7.26:1.4: Yāvakīvañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhū satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāvessanti; vuddhiyeva, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā, no parihāni.
root_text.an7.26:2.6: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāvessanti; vuddhiyeva, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā, no parihāni.
root_text.an7.26:3.1: Yāvakīvañca, bhikkhave, ime satta aparihāniyā dhammā bhikkhūsu ṭhassanti, imesu ca sattasu aparihāniyesu dhammesu bhikkhū sandississanti; vuddhiyeva, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ pāṭikaṅkhā, no parihānī”ti.

translation_text.an7.26:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you seven principles that prevent decline.
translation_text.an7.26:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well …
translation_text.an7.26:1.3: And what are the seven principles that prevent decline?
translation_text.an7.26:1.4: As long as the mendicants develop the awakening factor of mindfulness …
translation_text.an7.26:2.6: equanimity, they can expect growth, not decline.
translation_text.an7.26:3.1: As long as these seven principles that prevent decline last among the mendicants, and as long as the mendicants are seen following them, they can expect growth, not decline.”
